They confirmed the 2026-02-28 mutual termination of the northstar_legal Q1 2026 IR contingency retainer is on their record, but their AR system did not auto-generate a credit memo because the pre-bill had already converted to an invoice before the cancellation was entered in their billing module.\\n\\nTheir AR lead said they will issue a manual credit memo for VEN-030-736427 within five business days. She also noted they have seen the same pre-bill/cancellation miss for other clients when the cancellation is logged after the invoice has converted, so this appears to be a trigger-logic gap on the vendor side as well, not just a filing gap in our packet.\\n\\nGiven that confirmation, I agree the Brookfield-side disposition should stay stale void with the credit-memo backfill tracked to receipt. From my side, there is no tax wrinkle behind the stale alert.\\n\\nRyan can treat this as fully closed from a tax review standpoint.\\n\\nThanks,\\nHannah\", \"cc\": [\"ryan.delgado@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"alex.cahoon@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"edith.banda@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"parent_id\": \"email_scen_014_orphan_exception_0006\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-04-21T20:05:00Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"18da6bdab650491d81ed75452021d3ba\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"anaya.patel@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"peter.sanchez@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Final status check on filing calendar exception\", \"content\": \"Hi Peter,\\n\\nFollowing up on the filing calendar cleanup, I have the Northstar Legal annual report reminder and one other exception ready for final review. The stale auto-fire does not indicate any actual review work restarted on the item; it looks like reminder noise against the already closed case tied to the $380.50 timing difference and reconciliation BL-5FEFEA879504.\\n\\nNo substantive work reopened the case, and there is nothing further to push through the accounting records based on this alert.\\n\\nThanks,\\nJames\", \"cc\": [\"matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"jones.harrison@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"edith.banda@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"parent_id\": \"email_scen_013_orphan_exception_0006\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-03-24T17:43:00Z\", \"is_read\": true}]"}

Handing the file to Daniel for BD2 review.", "user": "persona_007", "reply_count": 2, "reply_users_count": 1, "latest_reply": "1778009520.000000", "client_msg_id": "scenario_scen_025_monthly_close_bd1_status_update_0"}, {"ts": "1777313863.310332", "type": "message", "text": "Quick question on the Northstar IOLTA tie-out: are we treating exc_151b0bee7e374e and exc_3e229b67ef6147 as the only blockers, or do any of the other open items still hit the trust ledger match?", "user": "persona_002", "reply_count": 1, "reply_users_count": 1, "latest_reply": "1777313922.615145"}, {"ts": "1775656800.000000", "type": "message", "text": "Starting the northstar Q1 partner package now using FP-2026-01, FP-2026-02, and FP-2026-03 as the base periods. I'm expecting two close-package adjusting JEs to hit FP-2026-04 before the 2026-05-05 lock, so I'll hold the package open for those final true-ups. Target cadence is Daniel Jones review on 2026-05-04 afternoon and Matthew Li sign-off by 2026-05-05 EOD if the April adjustments land on time.", "user": "persona_007", "client_msg_id": "scenario_scen_058_quarterly_reporting_quarterly_package_kickoff_post_0"}, {"ts": "1775499480.000000", "type": "message", "text": "From the Northstar engagement side this reads like a routine stale tickler on a case that was already closed, so I don't see client-facing risk here. Different fact pattern than the July accrued-comp item Harry handled; that one had an open presentation question, this one looks administrative if Daniel's prior sign-off still stands. Please copy me on the dismissal email so we have the audit trail tied off.", "user": "persona_023", "thread_ts": "1775487300.000000", "client_msg_id": "scenario_scen_015_orphan_exception_partner_gives_light_touch_ok_0"}, {"ts": "1775493000.000000", "type": "message", "text": "Pattern-wise, Northstar payroll-tax-withholding variances on 235000 have usually been IRS processor duplicate-confirmation noise rather than a live recon break. The October case was the $580-ish duplicate-confirm item Owen is talking about, and I also saw a similar January 2026 item on this same account that closed cleanly. Worth checking the prior thread and close support to see if this re-fire is following that same duplicate-confirm pattern.", "user": "persona_028", "thread_ts": "1775487300.000000", "client_msg_id": "scenario_scen_015_orphan_exception_peer_adds_pattern_context_0"}, {"ts": "1775487300.000000", "type": "message", "text": "Flagging exc_5221568d4e4047 in close coordination: this looks like a stale SLA on the already-closed Northstar payroll-tax-withholding feed-drop for $583.94 on account 235000 in FP-2025-10. George McAdam already confirmed it appears to be a re-fire rather than a live reopen. Daniel Jones, can you confirm dismissal sign-off still stands from the prior close? Matthew Li, any engagement-side concern if we treat this as a polling re-fire only? Reshma Patel, have you seen the same pattern on similar Northstar payroll-tax-withholding variances?", "user": "npc_029", "reply_count": 2, "reply_users_count": 2, "latest_reply": "1775499480.000000", "client_msg_id": "scenario_scen_015_orphan_exception_assignee_opens_coordination_thread_0"}, {"ts": "1774895458.493868", "type": "message", "text": "Quick clarifier on Northstar IOLTA: are we treating the dormant matter balances as reportable this month, or only the items still blocking the client trust ledger tie-out?", "user": "persona_002", "reply_count": 2, "reply_users_count": 2, "latest_reply": "1774895690.575594"}, {"ts": "1774295280.000000", "type": "message", "text": "Edith's framing is the right one. If we send any dismissal note, lead with BlackLine polling-bug auto-fire on the already closed item and do not imply anyone reopened July work. James Randall's August sign-off should still be treated as controlling here. Please keep the wording clean enough that it reads fine if Northstar's in-house accountant later asks why there was BlackLine activity against a July 2025 item.", "user": "persona_023", "thread_ts": "1774278480.000000", "client_msg_id": "scenario_scen_013_orphan_exception_partner_sets_presentability_bar_0"}, {"ts": "1774287000.000000", "type": "message", "text": "Northstar context: be careful how we frame this one. They get very sensitive about accrued-comp variances because account 225000 rolls into partner-attorney bonus-pool perception at quarter-end. We had questions before when unexplained activity showed up against a closed accrued-comp period, so if anything gets written up, lead with BlackLine polling auto-firing on an already closed item and avoid wording that sounds like we re-opened or re-investigated the July period.", "user": "persona_007", "thread_ts": "1774278480.000000", "client_msg_id": "scenario_scen_013_orphan_exception_peer_adds_fee_sensitivity_context_0"}, {"ts": "1774278480.000000", "type": "message", "text": "Stale SLA just re-fired on closed exception exc_1ec70e0d938b4f for Northstar Legal. This is the $380.50 item on account 225000 for period northstar_legal_FP-2025-07, tied to reconciliation BL-5FEFEA879504 and it was already closed August 7. Matthew Li, can you give partner eyes on any dismissal wording if we need to explain why this should be ignored? Edith Banda, before anything client-presentable gets drafted, can you share any Northstar-specific context or fee-sensitivity we should keep in mind?", "user": "persona_003", "reply_count": 2, "reply_users_count": 2, "latest_reply": "1774295280.000000", "client_msg_id": "scenario_scen_013_orphan_exception_assignee_flags_engagement_context_in_slack_0"}, {"ts": "1772652798.683022", "type": "message", "text": "One item to add: the multi-state return for the Northstar matter has a state apportionment memo issue, but it looks reviewable rather than a filing blocker. I would not escalate that unless the partner wants a position memo.", "user": "npc_025", "thread_ts": "1772652666.612777", "reply_count": 2, "reply_users_count": 2, "latest_reply": "1772652970.920260"}], "has_more": false}

input.command:
cd /home/agent/.claude/projects/-app/fbd2453f-fb4e-4399-b9a1-4c5fc1ef0eac/tool-results/ && python3 -c "
import json
d=json.load(open('mcp-mcp_bfca2b27-sap_subledger_list_ap_invoices-1781857391022.txt'))
r=json.loads(d['result'])
inv=r['ap_invoices']
print('total ap invoices:', r.get('total_ap_invoices'), 'returned:', len(inv))
from collections import Counter
print('status counts:', Counter(i['status'] for i in inv))
print()
print('--- PENDING_APPROVAL (aging) ---')
pend=[i for i in inv if i['status']=='pending_approval']
def age(i):
    return i.get('invoice_date') or i.get('time_received') or ''
for i in sorted(pend, key=age):
    print(i.get('invoice_date'), '|', i.get('vendor_name'), '|', i.get('invoice_number'), '|', i.get('id'),'| \$'+str(i.get('amount')), '|', i.get('gl_account_number'), '| recv', i.get('time_received'))
"
input.description: Summarize Northstar AP invoices by status and aging

content.text: Now I have the period and Airtable structure. Let me process the pending-AP file and pull the draft WIP JE, AR transaction detail, engagement-letter count, the latest trial balance, and Airtable records.

input.command:
cd /home/agent/.claude/projects/-app/fbd2453f-fb4e-4399-b9a1-4c5fc1ef0eac/tool-results/ && python3 -c "
import json
d=json.load(open('mcp-mcp_bfca2b27-sap_subledger_list_ap_invoices-1781857484842.txt'))
r=json.loads(d['result'])
inv=r['ap_invoices']
print('total pending matching:', r.get('total_ap_invoices'), 'returned:', len(inv))
from datetime import datetime
asof=datetime(2026,6,12)
def days(s):
    try: return (asof-datetime.fromisoformat(s[:10])).days
    except: return None
tot=0
rows=[]
for i in inv:
    inv_d=i.get('invoice_date') or ''
    a=days(inv_d)
    tot+=i.get('amount',0)
    rows.append((a, i.get('invoice_date','')[:10], i.get('vendor_name'), i.get('invoice_number'), i.get('id'), i.get('amount'), i.get('gl_account_number')))
print('TOTAL pending \$ (northstar):', round(tot,2))
print()
print('age | inv_date | vendor | vinv | id | amt | gl')
for r2 in sorted(rows, key=lambda x:-(x[0] or 0)):
    flag=' <== >90d' if (r2[0] or 0)>90 else ''
    cp=' [CROWNPEAK]' if 'CrownPeak' in (r2[2] or '') else ''
    print(r2[0],'d |',r2[1],'|',r2[2],'|',r2[3],'|',r2[4],'| \$'+str(r2[5]),'|',r2[6],flag,cp)
"

content.text: I have everything I need. Key cross-check: George previously flagged to Daniel that Acme Cloud ran ~15 hrs over its **engagement-letter baseline** and offered fee-impact detail — so the firm already manages EL overages, but for Northstar there's no EL covering the non-audit work at all. Let me now build the memo (computing AP aging buckets precisely), file it to Records Vault, create the Airtable record, and email George + Daniel.

input.command:
cd /home/agent/.claude/projects/-app/fbd2453f-fb4e-4399-b9a1-4c5fc1ef0eac/tool-results/ && python3 << 'EOF'
import json,base64
from datetime import datetime
asof=datetime(2026,6,12)
d=json.load(open('mcp-mcp_bfca2b27-sap_subledger_list_ap_invoices-1781857484842.txt'))
inv=json.loads(d['result'])['ap_invoices']
def days(s):
    try: return (asof-datetime.fromisoformat(s[:10])).days
    except: return 0
buckets={'0-30':[0,0],'31-60':[0,0],'61-90':[0,0],'91-180':[0,0],'181-365':[0,0]}
tot=0;cnt=0;over90=0;over90amt=0
for i in inv:
    a=days(i.get('invoice_date',''));amt=i.get('amount',0);tot+=amt;cnt+=1
    if a>90: over90+=1;over90amt+=amt
    for k,(lo) in [('0-30',(0,30)),]:
        pass
    if a<=30:b='0-30'
    elif a<=60:b='31-60'
    elif a<=90:b='61-90'
    elif a<=180:b='91-180'
    else:b='181-365'
    buckets[b][0]+=1;buckets[b][1]+=amt
print('AP pending total: $%.2f across %d invoices'%(tot,cnt))
print('>90 days: %d invoices, $%.2f'%(over90,over90amt))
for k,v in buckets.items():
    print('  %s days: %d inv, $%.2f'%(k,v[0],round(v[1],2)))
# crownpeak
cp=[i for i in inv if 'CrownPeak' in (i.get('vendor_name') or '')]
print('CrownPeak pending: %d, $%.2f'%(len(cp),sum(i['amount'] for i in cp)))
for i in sorted(cp,key=lambda x:-days(x.get('invoice_date',''))):
    print('  ',i['invoice_number'],i['id'],'$%.2f'%i['amount'],days(i.get('invoice_date',''))//1,'d gl',i['gl_account_number'])
EOF

cd /home/agent/.claude/projects/-app/fbd2453f-fb4e-4399-b9a1-4c5fc1ef0eac/tool-results/ && python3 << 'EOF'
import base64
memo = r"""BROOKFIELD CPAs & ADVISORS - ENGAGEMENT STATUS REVIEW (INTERNAL / PARTNER PREP)
Client / entity:  Northstar Legal LLP (northstar_legal)
Prepared by:      George McAdam, Accounts Senior
Prepared for:     Daniel Jones (partner) - prep for next Northstar discussion with Matthew Li
Date:             2026-06-12
Classification:   Restricted - contains fee, scope and independence analysis

====================================================================
1. EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
====================================================================
The Northstar engagement is healthy on day-to-day bookkeeping but carries four issues that
should be on the table before the next partner meeting:

  A. SCOPE / ENGAGEMENT LETTER GAP (highest priority). The ONLY engagement letter on file in
     Records Vault for Northstar is the FY2026 *Audit* Engagement Letter (executed 2026-05-11).
     There is no engagement letter on file covering the recurring bookkeeping/monthly close,
     management-accounts, FY2025 annual-accounts preparation, FY2025 tax preparation, or the
     advisory work we have actually been performing. That non-audit work is undocumented as to
     scope and fee, AND - because we are now the FY2026 auditor - it raises an independence /
     self-review concern that should be resolved before the audit advances.

  B. WIP DOES NOT TIE OUT. WIP (119000) of ~$2.01M has only ever been debited - it is recognised
     but never relieved against billings. It does not reconcile to what has been invoiced/approved,
     and the current-month WIP recognition entry is stuck in draft awaiting an hours tie-out.

  C. AR STALENESS / OVER-RESERVING. Gross AR (110000) is $647,359; the allowance for doubtful
     accounts (117000) is $1,057,728 - i.e. the allowance now EXCEEDS gross AR. Bad-debt expense
     is $427,019 YTD. Collectability of the aged book needs a partner view.

  D. AP BACKLOG. $302,063 of vendor invoices sit in pending_approval; 75 of 87 (~$274,917, 91% of
     value) are over 90 days old, and several CrownPeak dispositions agreed in comms were never
     executed in SAP.

====================================================================
2. ACCOUNTS RECEIVABLE - WHAT IS OUTSTANDING, HOW OLD, STALENESS RISK
====================================================================
(All figures from northstar_legal GL - the books we maintain.)
  - Gross AR (110000): $647,359.49 at FP-2026-04 close (unchanged into the open FP-2026-05).
  - Trend: AR has fallen from $2,182,077 (Jul-2025 close) to $647,359 (Apr-2026) - down ~70% YoY.
    The AR sub-ledger shows regular billing ("Litigation engagement revenue realization") and
    regular client payments through April 2026, so the active book is turning over.
  - STALENESS SIGNALS:
      * Allowance for doubtful accounts (117000) = $1,057,727.96 (credit), which is ~$410K MORE
        than gross AR. An allowance larger than the gross receivable is an anomaly: either the
        reserve is overstated or write-offs are lagging the reserve. Net AR is effectively a
        net credit position - this needs partner review for presentation and reserve adequacy.
      * Bad-debt expense (590000) YTD = $427,018.64, and the allowance grew $86,799 in FP-2026-04
        alone - indicating a recurring slice of the book is being treated as uncollectible.
  - LIMITATION: a clean invoice-level aging bucket (30/60/90/120+) for AR is not reconstructable
    from the GL/sub-ledger as held; the staleness conclusion is drawn from the allowance, the
    bad-debt run-rate, and the YoY decline. Recommend pulling a client-level AR aging from the
    practice/billing system to confirm which matters are driving the reserve.

====================================================================
3. WORK IN PROCESS (WIP) - DOES IT TIE TO INVOICED & APPROVED?
====================================================================
  - WIP (119000) current balance: $2,013,942.79 (FP-2026-04 close $1,914,360.46).
  - FINDING - WIP is never relieved: in every period reviewed, account 119000 has DEBITS ONLY and
    ZERO credits (e.g. FP-2025-07 debits $1,145,422 / credits $0; FP-2026-04 debits $109,889 /
    credits $0). WIP recognition entries are DR 119000 WIP / CR 401000 Revenue, but no entry ever
    relieves WIP when the work is billed. Billings are booked separately as DR 110000 AR /
    CR 401000 Revenue.
  - CONSEQUENCES:
      * WIP does NOT tie to what has been invoiced/approved - there is no relief mechanism, so the
        $2.01M balance simply accumulates and is disconnected from billings/collections.
      * Revenue double-count risk: because both WIP recognition and billing realization credit
        revenue (401000) and WIP is never reversed at billing, revenue appears to be recognised
        twice on the same work. This should be verified and, if confirmed, corrected.
  - The current WIP recognition entry is BLOCKED: JE-northstar_legal-FP-2026-05-0001
    (je_d3072fe78df54c5b), $34,495.06, DR 119000 / CR 401000, prepared by William White, is still
    in DRAFT. Per the close-channel thread it is held pending the billable-hours tie-out (hours
    file owed by Emily Adekole / Anaya Patel); the team noted it would be "marked pending client
    input and the WIP posting held." FP-2026-05 is still OPEN (BD5 close was 2026-06-05, now past).

====================================================================
4. SCOPE vs ENGAGEMENT LETTER
====================================================================
  - On file (Records Vault, exactly ONE engagement letter for Northstar):
      * doc_0036f5b991574808 - "Northstar Legal FY2026 Audit Engagement Letter - Executed 2026-05-11"
      * Supporting: FY2026 Audit Materiality Memo (2026-05-18) - audit fieldwork is starting.
  - Work actually performed for Northstar that is NOT covered by any engagement letter on file:
      * Monthly bookkeeping & close (ongoing all FY; FP-2026-04 closed, FP-2026-05 in progress).
      * Management-accounts packages (e.g. FP-2026-04 Management Accounts Package).
      * FY2025 annual-accounts preparation (working file, IOLTA/operating-cash recs, fixed-asset
        rollforward, prepaid schedule; ASC-710 partner-draw reclass $18,750; SALT adjustment
        $4,820.30; finalised ~May 2026).
      * FY2025 federal + state tax preparation (Form 1065, K-1s, M-1, SALT; data package filed
        2026-06-08).
      * Advisory: partner-distribution tax-cadence modelling projecting ~$180K aggregate tax
        savings across the eight partners; AML/sanctions monitoring (AML-REG-northstar-2025-001).
  - TWO distinct problems:
      1. Undocumented scope/fee: a full book of recurring + advisory services is being delivered
         with no signed engagement letter or fee schedule on file. This is exactly the kind of
         out-of-scope-without-documentation exposure that should be papered with a change order
         or a separate accounting/advisory engagement letter.
      2. Independence / self-review (the bigger partner issue): we are the FY2026 auditor while
         also keeping the books, preparing the management accounts, preparing the tax return, and
         providing tax-planning advisory for the same client. Under the AICPA independence rules
         these create self-review and management-participation threats that impair independence
         absent significant safeguards. This needs a partner decision before the audit progresses.
  - Precedent for handling: on Acme Cloud, George flagged to Daniel (2026-05-04) that the MMA wrap
    ran ~15 hours over the engagement-letter baseline (state sales-tax coding bleeding into close)
    and offered hour detail for a fee-impact discussion. Northstar is more fundamental - there is
    no engagement letter covering the non-audit work at all, so the baseline itself is undocumented.

  RECOMMENDATION: Daniel to decide whether to (a) absorb the non-audit work, or (b) take a change
  order / separate accounting & advisory engagement letter to Matthew Li - and separately confirm
  the independence safeguards (or re-scoping) needed given the concurrent FY2026 audit.

====================================================================
5. ACCOUNTS PAYABLE - AGED VENDOR INVOICES (books we maintain for Northstar)
====================================================================
  - Pending_approval total: $302,063.22 across 87 invoices.
      * 0-30 days:   0 inv,  $0
      * 31-60 days:  5 inv,  $13,886.89
      * 61-90 days:  7 inv,  $13,259.55
      * 91-180 days: 25 inv, $97,514.95
      * 181-365 days:50 inv, $177,401.83
      * OVER 90 DAYS: 75 of 87 invoices = $274,916.78 (91% of the pending value).
  - This is a structural AP-approval backlog, not a CrownPeak-only issue. Largest aged items
    include Stonepath $16,213.76 (137d), VaultSpan $12,896.12 (250d), ArchiveSpring $11,786.59
    (130d), BlueMesa $10,998.09 (324d), Northbar $10,299.62 (208d), LexHarbor $9,863.97 (325d),
    Ridgewell $8,547.33 (322d), VerityTrail $8,783.26 (225d), CivicGrid $8,174.82 (177d),
    Cobalt Harbor $10,866.53 (96d).
  - CrownPeak Cyber Defense cluster - 5 invoices, $8,260.52:
      * VEN-030-817856 / apinv_178dc009be6c474f - $1,152.41 - 339d - mis-coded to 210000, should be
        219000; review concluded "re-code and release" (Linear in_progress) - NOT yet executed.
      * VEN-030-990998 / apinv_a024c38fca6740ca - $5,255.97 - 197d - long-pending, NOT addressed in
        any comms found.
      * VEN-030-989543 / apinv_3511b5d2910e4821 - $969.98 - 203d - long-pending, NOT addressed in
        any comms found.
      * VEN-030-353041 / apinv_e0235997b4964a8f - $459.84 - 95d - confirmed duplicate of paid
        VEN-030-353009; Matthew Li gave a narrative dismissal, but the invoice is STILL
        pending_approval in SAP (never voided).
      * VEN-030-736427 / apinv_e42396e9bdd64294 - $422.32 - 93d - stale pre-bill on the Q1-2026 IR
        retainer cancelled 2026-02-28; Matthew approved the void and a vendor credit memo was to be
        backfilled, but the invoice is STILL pending_approval in SAP (never voided).

====================================================================
6. DOES THE COMMS STORY MATCH THE NUMBERS?
====================================================================
  - PARTLY. The CrownPeak follow-through reads as resolved in Slack/email/Linear (duplicate
    dismissed, stale pre-bill approved for void, credit memo coming). In SAP, however, the two
    dispositioned items (353041 duplicate; 736427 stale pre-bill) remain in pending_approval - the
    agreed void/dismissal was never executed. Two further CrownPeak items (990998 $5,255.97;
    989543 $969.98), both ~200 days old, do not appear in any thread - they are aging silently.
  - The annual-accounts and tax workstreams reconcile to the GL (FY2025 reclasses and the SALT/
    partner-draw adjustments tie to the late-posts referenced in email).
  - The scope question is where comms and records diverge most: the comms treat the bookkeeping,
    accounts, tax and advisory work as routine BAU, but the engagement records contain only an
    AUDIT engagement letter to support any of it.

====================================================================
7. OPEN LINEAR / AIRTABLE ITEMS TOUCHING NORTHSTAR (not closed)
====================================================================
  Linear (all overdue):
    - issue_dfa400bcc1314b07a2c85aeaea3bf746 - CrownPeak coding clarification (VEN-030-817856
      re-code 210000->219000) - in_progress, due 2026-05-27.
    - issue_ba662bdea4584e1f949f58f5f49e3793 - Document CrownPeak IR coding-helper rule - in_progress,
      due 2026-05-27.
    - issue_0ab11dc60f2541ffa44cfb183c02c18a - Append VEN-030-736427 stale-void write-up - in_progress,
      due 2025-10-03.
    - issue_05de9a8fc05c4effb88c2797c882ce41 - Review CrownPeak AP queue / tune duplicate detection -
      todo, due 2026-06-02.
    - issue_7e7797e4560f4f07b0b936d4dff032bb - CrownPeak duplicate-detection partner sign-off
      conditions - todo, due 2026-06-02.
    (issue_70fdb3e2b126483d9d6d8b8d39eaf48a - "close out CrownPeak three-item arc" is marked done,
     but the underlying SAP items are still open - the tracker is ahead of reality.)
  Airtable: no existing Northstar-specific row in the Close Blocker Triage Log or AP Workflow
    Exceptions bases; an engagement-status record has been created in the Close Blocker Triage Log
    to reflect this review.

====================================================================
8. RECOMMENDED ACTIONS FOR THE PARTNER MEETING
====================================================================
  1. Scope/fee + independence: decide absorb vs. change order for the non-audit work, and paper a
     separate accounting & advisory engagement letter; confirm independence safeguards given the
     concurrent FY2026 audit (or re-scope who does the books vs. the audit).
  2. WIP: investigate why 119000 is never relieved and confirm whether revenue is double-counted;
     unblock JE-northstar_legal-FP-2026-05-0001 by getting the billable-hours tie-out, then close
     FP-2026-05.
  3. AR: review reserve adequacy/presentation (allowance > gross AR) and pull a client-level aging.
  4. AP: clear the >90-day backlog ($274,917); execute the two CrownPeak dispositions already
     approved (void 353041 and 736427) and triage the two un-discussed CrownPeak items
     (990998, 989543); re-code and release 817856.
"""
open('northstar_status_memo.txt','w').write(memo)
b64=base64.b64encode(memo.encode()).decode()
open('northstar_memo_b64.txt','w').write(b64)
print('memo chars:',len(memo),'b64 chars:',len(b64))
EOF

input.subject: Northstar engagement - full status review (AR / WIP / scope / AP) for partner prep
input.content:
Daniel,

Following your steer this morning, here is the full picture on Northstar across the books, the engagement records, and the comms - structured so you can walk into the Matthew meeting without surprises. Full memo with all supporting detail is filed in Records Vault (doc_de12ec7b285f, restricted, AICPA_SQMS_7Y) and an engagement-status record is logged in Airtable / Close Blocker Triage Log (recd509a0f8f02348).

Four things are worth raising; one of them (scope/independence) is the headline.

=====================================================================
HEADLINE - SCOPE vs ENGAGEMENT LETTER (decide before the audit advances)
=====================================================================
The ONLY engagement letter on file in Records Vault for Northstar is the FY2026 AUDIT Engagement Letter (executed 2026-05-11). There is NO engagement letter covering the work we are actually doing day to day:
  - monthly bookkeeping & close,
  - management-accounts packages,
  - FY2025 annual-accounts preparation (working file, recs, partner-draw reclass $18,750, SALT adj $4,820.30),
  - FY2025 federal + state tax prep (Form 1065/K-1s/M-1/SALT, package filed 2026-06-08),
  - advisory (partner-distribution tax-cadence modelling projecting ~$180K of savings; AML/sanctions monitoring).

Two problems flow from that:
  1. Undocumented scope/fee - a whole book of recurring + advisory services with no signed engagement letter or fee schedule behind it. This is exactly the out-of-scope-without-documentation exposure to paper with a change order or a separate accounting & advisory letter.
  2. Independence / self-review - because we are now the FY2026 auditor while also keeping the books, preparing the management accounts, preparing the tax return and giving tax-planning advice, AICPA independence rules raise self-review and management-participation threats. This needs a partner decision before the audit progresses.

(Precedent: on Acme Cloud, George flagged the MMA wrap running ~15 hrs over the EL baseline and offered hour detail for fee impact. Northstar is more fundamental - there is no EL covering the non-audit work at all, so there is no baseline to measure against.)

DECISION FOR YOU: absorb the non-audit work, or take a change order / separate accounting & advisory engagement letter to Matthew - and separately confirm the independence safeguards (or re-scope who does the books vs the audit).

=====================================================================
WIP - DOES NOT TIE TO INVOICED/APPROVED
=====================================================================
  - WIP (119000) = $2,013,942.79.
  - It is never relieved: account 119000 has debits only and zero credits in every period (e.g. FP-2025-07 $1,145,422 dr / $0 cr; FP-2026-04 $109,889 dr / $0 cr). WIP recognition is DR WIP / CR Revenue, but nothing reverses WIP when work is billed - billings post separately as DR AR / CR Revenue.
  - Consequence: WIP does NOT reconcile to what has been invoiced/approved; it just accumulates. Because both WIP recognition and billing realization credit revenue and WIP is never reversed, there is a revenue double-count risk that should be verified and, if confirmed, corrected.
  - The current WIP recognition entry, JE-northstar_legal-FP-2026-05-0001 (je_d3072fe78df54c5b, $34,495.06, prepared by William White), is still in DRAFT, held pending the billable-hours tie-out (hours file owed by Emily Adekole / Anaya Patel). FP-2026-05 is still OPEN (BD5 was 2026-06-05).

=====================================================================
AR - WHAT'S OUTSTANDING, HOW OLD, STALENESS RISK
=====================================================================
  - Gross AR (110000) = $647,359.49, down from $2,182,077 (Jul-2025) - ~70% YoY. The sub-ledger shows regular billing and client payments through April, so the active book turns over.
  - STALENESS FLAG: the allowance for doubtful accounts (117000) is $1,057,727.96 - it EXCEEDS gross AR by ~$410K. Bad-debt expense is $427,018.64 YTD and the allowance grew $86,799 in April alone. An allowance larger than gross AR is an anomaly (over-reserved or write-offs lagging) and needs a partner view on reserve adequacy and presentation.
  - Caveat: a clean invoice-level aging (30/60/90/120+) isn't reconstructable from the GL as held - recommend pulling a client-level AR aging from the billing system to confirm which matters drive the reserve.

=====================================================================
AP - AGED VENDOR INVOICES (the queue you heard about)
=====================================================================
  - $302,063.22 sits in pending_approval across 87 invoices. 75 of 87 (= $274,916.78, 91% of value) are over 90 days old; 50 are over 180 days. This is a structural approval backlog, not just CrownPeak.
  - Largest aged items: Stonepath $16,213.76 (137d), VaultSpan $12,896.12 (250d), ArchiveSpring $11,786.59 (130d), BlueMesa $10,998.09 (324d), Cobalt Harbor $10,866.53 (96d), Northbar $10,299.62 (208d), LexHarbor $9,863.97 (325d), Ridgewell $8,547.33 (322d), VerityTrail $8,783.26 (225d), CivicGrid $8,174.82 (177d).
  - CrownPeak cluster (5 invoices, $8,260.52): 817856 $1,152.41 (339d, re-code 210000->219000 & release - not done); 990998 $5,255.97 (197d) and 989543 $969.98 (203d) - both aging and NOT discussed anywhere; 353041 $459.84 (95d, confirmed duplicate, Matthew gave narrative dismissal) and 736427 $422.32 (93d, stale pre-bill, Matthew approved void) - BOTH still pending_approval in SAP, never executed.

=====================================================================
DOES THE COMMS STORY MATCH THE NUMBERS?
=====================================================================
  - PARTLY. The CrownPeak follow-through reads as resolved in Slack/email/Linear, but in SAP the two dispositioned items (353041, 736427) are still open - the agreed void/dismissal was never executed - and two further CrownPeak items (990998, 989543, ~200d) never appear in any thread. The Linear card "close out CrownPeak three-item arc" is marked done while the underlying SAP items are still open - the tracker is ahead of reality.
  - The annual-accounts and tax workstreams DO reconcile to the GL (FY2025 reclasses and the SALT/partner-draw late-posts tie out).
  - The biggest divergence is scope: comms treat the bookkeeping/accounts/tax/advisory work as routine BAU, but the records contain only an audit engagement letter to support any of it.

=====================================================================
OPEN LINEAR ITEMS (all overdue, all Northstar/CrownPeak)
=====================================================================
  - issue_dfa400bcc1314b07a2c85aeaea3bf746 - CrownPeak re-code 817856 (in_progress, due 2026-05-27)
  - issue_ba662bdea4584e1f949f58f5f49e3793 - CrownPeak IR coding-helper rule (in_progress, due 2026-05-27)
  - issue_0ab11dc60f2541ffa44cfb183c02c18a - VEN-030-736427 stale-void write-up (in_progress, due 2025-10-03)
  - issue_05de9a8fc05c4effb88c2797c882ce41 - Review CrownPeak AP queue / dup detection (todo, due 2026-06-02)
  - issue_7e7797e4560f4f07b0b936d4dff032bb - CrownPeak dup-detection sign-off conditions (todo, due 2026-06-02)
No Northstar-specific Airtable row existed; I created the engagement-status record noted above.

=====================================================================
RECOMMENDED ACTIONS
=====================================================================
  1. Scope/fee + independence: decide absorb vs change order, paper a separate accounting & advisory engagement letter, and confirm independence safeguards given the concurrent FY2026 audit.
  2. WIP: investigate why 119000 is never relieved / confirm whether revenue is double-counted; get the hours tie-out, post je_d3072fe78df54c5b, and close FP-2026-05.
  3. AR: review reserve adequacy/presentation (allowance > gross AR) and pull a client-level aging.
  4. AP: clear the >90-day backlog ($274,917); execute the two already-approved CrownPeak voids (353041, 736427); triage the two undiscussed items (990998, 989543); re-code and release 817856.

Happy to pull any of the underlying detail (full AP list, AR/WIP transaction trail, the draft JE) ahead of the meeting.

George

content.text:
Done. I went through the entire Northstar engagement across the GL, sub-ledgers, engagement records, comms, Linear and Airtable, and pulled it together for Daniel's partner prep. Here's where things actually stand:

## The four things Daniel needs to know

**1. Scope / engagement letter (the headline).** The *only* engagement letter on file in Records Vault is the **FY2026 Audit** letter (executed 2026-05-11). Nothing on file covers the work we're actually doing — monthly bookkeeping/close, management accounts, FY2025 annual-accounts prep, FY2025 tax prep (Form 1065), or the advisory work (the ~$180K partner tax-cadence model; AML monitoring). That's two problems: (a) a full book of recurring + advisory work with no signed scope/fee behind it, and (b) an **independence/self-review concern** — we can't comfortably audit FY2026 while also keeping the books, preparing the management accounts and tax return, and advising. This is the absorb-vs-change-order decision, plus an independence call.

**2. WIP doesn't tie out.** WIP (119000) is **$2.01M and has only ever been debited — never relieved**. WIP is recognised (DR WIP/CR revenue) but billings post separately (DR AR/CR revenue) with no relief, so WIP just accumulates and doesn't reconcile to what's invoiced — and there's a **revenue double-count risk**. The current WIP recognition JE ($34,495, `je_d3072fe78df54c5b`) is stuck in draft pending the hours tie-out; May (FP-2026-05) is still open past BD5.

**3. AR staleness.** Gross AR $647K (down ~70% YoY) but the **allowance ($1.06M) now exceeds gross AR**, with $427K bad debt YTD — over-reserved / collectability needs a partner view. (A clean invoice-level aging needs the billing system; flagged as a limitation.)

**4. AP backlog.** $302K pending approval, **75 of 87 invoices (91% of value, $275K) over 90 days**. The CrownPeak story in Slack/Linear reads "handled," but in SAP the two dispositioned items (353041 duplicate, 736427 stale pre-bill) were **never voided**, and two more CrownPeak items (~$6.2K, ~200 days) aren't discussed anywhere — comms are ahead of reality.

## What I actioned
- **Records Vault:** filed the full memo with supporting detail — `doc_de12ec7b285f` (kind: memo, classification: restricted, retention AICPA_SQMS_7Y).
- **Airtable:** created engagement-status record `recd509a0f8f02348` in the Close Blocker Triage Log (no Northstar row existed; High / Escalated).
- **Email:** sent the full summary to you and Daniel (`83a2dc...`).

One judgment call worth flagging: the AR/WIP/AP figures are from **Northstar's own ledger (the books we keep)**; Brookfield's own engagement WIP isn't separately carved out in the data, so I was explicit in the memo about whose numbers are whose. If you want, I can pull a true client-level AR aging or the full WIP transaction trail before the meeting.
